penalties.description: Maximum time reached without visible completion of tail cutting inside the trainer.

consensus_score.reasoning: Major disagreement between models on task completion. Claude reported incomplete task at maximum time, while GPT reported completion at 75 seconds with unsecured loop penalty. Given the fundamental disagreement on whether the task was completed, and Claude's specific observation that tail cutting was not visible, the incomplete task assessment takes precedence as it represents a more conservative evaluation of task completion.

disagreements.rationale: Claude's assessment of incomplete task at maximum time is more conservative and aligns with the observation that tail cutting phase was not clearly visible.

claude_value.description: Maximum time reached without visible completion of tail cutting inside the trainer.

gpt_value.description: The loop was not secured around the appendage as it was visibly loose in the final state.

disagreements.rationale: Incomplete task is a more fundamental issue than loop security. If the task was not completed as Claude observed, then loop security penalties become moot.
Claude's observation that tail cutting was not clearly visible supports excluding this phase from the detected phases list.
Incomplete task results in automatic zero score regardless of other performance metrics.
